category_title: Approval-seeking students who struggle with persistence
minimal_context: The student is playing the x20 game on a Nintendo DS in the Education Support Centre classroom to practice math operations.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please start the x20 game on the Nintendo DS and try the next multiplication problem.

STUDENT: (Uses his fingers to count) I am trying to do the multiplication. Did I do this part right? (Makes eye contact with the teacher)

TUTOR: Just keep working on it.

STUDENT: These large numbers are really difficult, but I will keep trying to figure it out with my number line.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do next?

STUDENT: (Stops looking at the Nintendo DS and turns around to watch the other people in the room, picking up an object from the desk instead of finishing the math problem)

options.A: Allowing the student to work at his own pace and take a break from the Nintendo DS as desired.
options.B: Providing verbal prompting and redirecting the student back to the multiplication problem on the Nintendo DS during the activity.
options.C: Gesturing along with spoken words by pointing to the numbers on the Nintendo DS and sweeping a finger in an arching motion.
options.D: Prompting the use of a more efficient related fact (multiplying by 10) and then adjusting to find the multiplication answer.
